root_text.an3.116:1.3: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco puggalo sabbaso rūpasaññānaṁ samatikkamā paṭighasaññānaṁ atthaṅgamā nānattasaññānaṁ amanasikārā ‘ananto ākāso’ti ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an3.116:1.4: So tadassādeti taṁ nikāmeti tena ca vittiṁ āpajjati, tatra ṭhito tadadhimutto tabbahulavihārī aparihīno kālaṁ kurumāno ākāsānañcāyatanūpagānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an3.116:1.5: Ākāsānañcāyatanūpagānaṁ, bhikkhave, devānaṁ vīsati kappasahassāni āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an3.116:1.6: Tattha puthujjano yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā nirayampi gacchati tiracchānayonimpi gacchati pettivisayampi gacchati.
root_text.an3.116:1.7: Bhagavato pana sāvako tattha yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā tasmiṁyeva bhave parinibbāyati.
root_text.an3.116:1.8: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, viseso ayaṁ adhippayāso idaṁ nānākaraṇaṁ sutavato ariyasāvakassa assutavatā puthujjanena, yadidaṁ gatiyā upapattiyā.
root_text.an3.116:2.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo sabbaso ākāsānañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma ‘anantaṁ viññāṇan’ti viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an3.116:2.2: So tadassādeti taṁ nikāmeti tena ca vittiṁ āpajjati, tatra ṭhito tadadhimutto tabbahulavihārī aparihīno kālaṁ kurumāno viññāṇañcāyatanūpagānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an3.116:2.3: Viññāṇañcāyatanūpagānaṁ, bhikkhave, devānaṁ cattārīsaṁ kappasahassāni āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an3.116:2.4: Tattha puthujjano yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā nirayampi gacchati tiracchānayonimpi gacchati pettivisayampi gacchati.
root_text.an3.116:2.5: Bhagavato pana sāvako tattha yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā tasmiṁyeva bhave parinibbāyati.
root_text.an3.116:2.6: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, viseso ayaṁ adhippayāso idaṁ nānākaraṇaṁ sutavato ariyasāvakassa assutavatā puthujjanena, yadidaṁ gatiyā upapattiyā.
root_text.an3.116:3.1: Puna caparaṁ, bhikkhave, idhekacco puggalo sabbaso viññāṇañcāyatanaṁ samatikkamma ‘natthi kiñcī’ti ākiñcaññāyatanaṁ upasampajja viharati.
root_text.an3.116:3.2: So tadassādeti taṁ nikāmeti tena ca vittiṁ āpajjati, tatra ṭhito tadadhimutto tabbahulavihārī aparihīno kālaṁ kurumāno ākiñcaññāyatanūpagānaṁ devānaṁ sahabyataṁ upapajjati.
root_text.an3.116:3.3: Ākiñcaññāyatanūpagānaṁ, bhikkhave, devānaṁ saṭṭhi kappasahassāni āyuppamāṇaṁ.
root_text.an3.116:3.4: Tattha puthujjano yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā nirayampi gacchati tiracchānayonimpi gacchati pettivisayampi gacchati.
root_text.an3.116:3.5: Bhagavato pana sāvako tattha yāvatāyukaṁ ṭhatvā yāvatakaṁ tesaṁ devānaṁ āyuppamāṇaṁ taṁ sabbaṁ khepetvā tasmiṁyeva bhave parinibbāyati.
root_text.an3.116:3.6: Ayaṁ kho, bhikkhave, viseso, ayaṁ adhippayāso idaṁ nānākaraṇaṁ sutavato ariyasāvakassa assutavatā puthujjanena, yadidaṁ gatiyā upapattiyā.
root_text.an3.116:3.7: Ime kho, bhikkhave, tayo puggalā santo saṁvijjamānā lokasmin”ti.

translation_text.an3.116:1.1: “Mendicants, these three individuals are found in the world.
translation_text.an3.116:1.3: First, an individual, going totally beyond perceptions of form, with the disappearance of perceptions of impingement, not focusing on perceptions of diversity, aware that ‘space is infinite’, enters and remains in the dimension of infinite space.
translation_text.an3.116:1.4: They enjoy it and like it and find it rewarding. If they’re set on that, committed to it, and meditate on it often without losing it, when they die they’re reborn in the company of the gods of the dimension of infinite space.
translation_text.an3.116:1.5: The lifespan of the gods of infinite space is twenty thousand eons.
translation_text.an3.116:1.6: An ordinary person stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they go to hell or the animal realm or the ghost realm.
translation_text.an3.116:1.7: But a disciple of the Buddha stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they’re extinguished in that very life.
translation_text.an3.116:1.8: This is the difference between a learned noble disciple and an unlearned ordinary person as regards their place of rebirth.
translation_text.an3.116:2.1: Furthermore, an individual, going totally beyond the dimension of infinite space, aware that ‘consciousness is infinite’, enters and remains in the dimension of infinite consciousness.
translation_text.an3.116:2.2: They enjoy it and like it and find it rewarding. If they’re set on that, committed to it, and meditate on it often without losing it, when they die they’re reborn in the company of the gods of the dimension of infinite consciousness.
translation_text.an3.116:2.3: The lifespan of the gods of infinite consciousness is forty thousand eons.
translation_text.an3.116:2.4: An ordinary person stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they go to hell or the animal realm or the ghost realm.
translation_text.an3.116:2.5: But a disciple of the Buddha stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they’re extinguished in that very life.
translation_text.an3.116:2.6: This is the difference between a learned noble disciple and an unlearned ordinary person as regards their place of rebirth.
translation_text.an3.116:3.1: Furthermore, an individual, going totally beyond the dimension of infinite consciousness, aware that ‘there is nothing at all’, enters and remains in the dimension of nothingness.
translation_text.an3.116:3.2: They enjoy it and like it and find it rewarding. If they’re set on that, committed to it, and meditate on it often without losing it, when they die they’re reborn in the company of the gods of the dimension of nothingness.
translation_text.an3.116:3.3: The lifespan of the gods of nothingness is sixty thousand eons.
translation_text.an3.116:3.4: An ordinary person stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they go to hell or the animal realm or the ghost realm.
translation_text.an3.116:3.5: But a disciple of the Buddha stays there until the lifespan of those gods is spent, then they’re extinguished in that very life.
translation_text.an3.116:3.6: This is the difference between a learned noble disciple and an unlearned ordinary person as regards their place of rebirth.
translation_text.an3.116:3.7: These are the three individuals found in the world.”
